pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Edge-domain Operations & Program Management KSA with insufficient signal strength (composite score 0.423) to justify inclusion for the Director of AI/ML. Without stronger framework alignment, this knowledge KSA does not meet the inclusion threshold.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.challenge: This Knowledge KSA was excluded as an edge case, yet it has a composite score of 0.423 (moderate signal). Knowledge-dimension KSAs represent conceptual awareness rather than execution; exclusion may be too aggressive.
pass_3_adversarial_internal.rebuttal: Edge-domain Knowledge KSAs require more than moderate signal to justify inclusion; the role's scope does not regularly engage with Operations & Program Management. Exclusion upheld.
pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: Upheld: edge-domain exclusion maintained. Operations & Program Management is not sufficiently central to the Director of AI/ML to warrant inclusion at this signal level.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Could this edge-domain knowledge KSA be expected in real-world Director of AI/ML roles, even if not core to the role definition?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Recent job postings for Director of AI/ML roles increasingly include Operations & Program Management competencies as organizations broaden role scope. External evidence supports inclusion.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Overturn: evolving market expectations for Director of AI/ML now include Operations & Program Management competencies. Revised to include.

decision_rationale: Initially excluded but overturned through adversarial review. Operations & Program Management knowledge KSA is relevant to the Director of AI/ML based on external evidence and internal consistency re-evaluation.
